surface.name: Marqeta Core API Quick Start through a simulated transaction
surface.selection_basis: The documentation home promotes Core API Quick Start as the way to try the APIs, and the Platform Overview directs developers to that guide to get started.

alternatives_considered.name: Developer Tools
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This supports credentials and transaction inspection but directs developers back to Core API Quick Start for development.

alternatives_considered.name: Dashboard Overview and Quick Start
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route describes live Dashboard access and administrator-controlled programs rather than the self-serve public sandbox.

entry_point.developer_intent: Follow Marqeta's promoted Core API Quick Start from no account through a simulated sandbox transaction.
assumed_prior_state: No public sandbox or API credentials

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer simulates the documented $10 VISA authorization in the public sandbox and can view that card transaction in Transaction Timeline.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The simulation request completes and Transaction Timeline displays the transaction history for the selected active card token.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Step 3: Transact, simulate the authorization of a $10 transaction

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Core API Quick Start explicitly teaches sandbox transaction simulation after the required account, sandbox, user, card-product, and card setup; webhook and spend-control work is presented under Next steps.

prerequisites.requirement: Provide an email address, first name, and last name for the free Marqeta.com account.
Create and confirm a password of at least 16 characters with lowercase and uppercase letters, a number, and a symbol; agree to the API Terms of Service and complete the displayed reCAPTCHA.
Verify the account email, sign in again, and reach a ready public sandbox with Dashboard access.
Use the signed-in documentation widgets and the Dashboard-provided sample cURL to make the documented requests.

primary_path.action: Open Marqeta documentation and select Core API Quick Start.
primary_path.success_signal: Core API Quick Start is open.

primary_path.action: Open the official account-creation link from Core API Quick Start.
primary_path.output: The Create your Marqeta account form
primary_path.success_signal: The form displays identity, password, consent, and reCAPTCHA controls.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The page requires JavaScript to render the account client.

primary_path.action: Enter email, first name, last name, password, and password confirmation; choose whether to receive news, accept the API Terms of Service, complete reCAPTCHA, and select Create account.
details: The password must contain lowercase and uppercase letters, a number, a symbol, and at least 16 characters.
The news-and-updates checkbox is optional and selected by default; Terms of Service acceptance is required.
primary_path.input: Identity, password, consent, and CAPTCHA response
primary_path.success_signal: The page says a verification email will arrive in a few minutes.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid input returns an account-creation error; the public client does not enumerate every server-side validation message.

primary_path.action: Open the Marqeta account-verification email and follow its activation link.
primary_path.success_signal: The activation page says the email has been verified and offers Return to sign in.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account client says the verification email may take a few minutes; invalid or unauthorized activation tokens produce an error.

primary_path.action: Return to sign in and authenticate with the newly created account credentials.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer returns to Marqeta and can access the public sandbox flow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unverified account is told to confirm its email; Marqeta can issue additional sign-in challenges when configured.

candidate_paths.name: Explicit Create sandbox, signed-in widgets, and simulation cURL
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This candidate follows the Quick Start's explicit Create sandbox instruction, then uses the guide's signed-in widgets for asset creation and its required simulation cURL for the transaction.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Repeats the shared account and sign-in spine, then resolves the contradictory sandbox-provisioning language by following the separately enumerated Create sandbox section.

steps.action: Open Core API Quick Start and select its account sign-up link.
steps.failure_or_wait: JavaScript is required to render the form.

steps.action: Enter email, first name, last name, a conforming password, and matching password confirmation.
details: Password rules: lowercase and uppercase letters, a number, a symbol, and at least 16 characters.
steps.success_signal: All required identity and password fields are populated without validation errors.
steps.failure_or_wait: Invalid email, weak password, or mismatched confirmation prevents successful submission.

steps.action: Choose the optional news setting, accept the API Terms of Service, complete reCAPTCHA, and select Create account.
details: News and updates is selected by default and can be cleared.
Terms of Service acceptance is required.
steps.success_signal: Marqeta says an account-verification email will arrive in a few minutes.
steps.failure_or_wait: The account client reports invalid input or a create-account error if submission fails.

steps.success_signal: The verification message arrives at the submitted address.
steps.failure_or_wait: The account client says delivery can take a few minutes and provides a resend-activation-email route.

steps.action: Open the activation link, wait for Marqeta to validate the token, then select Return to sign in.
steps.success_signal: The page states that the email has been verified.
steps.failure_or_wait: Invalid or unauthorized activation tokens produce an error.

steps.action: Sign in again with the verified account credentials.
steps.success_signal: The public sandbox creation flow is available.
steps.failure_or_wait: Unverified email is rejected; configured accounts can receive an additional sign-in challenge.

steps.success_signal: Marqeta begins creating the public sandbox.
steps.failure_or_wait: The separate account guidance also says signup creates a sandbox and user, so this action may already have been satisfied; the conflict is preserved as an uncertainty.

steps.action: Provision the public sandbox, send the readiness email, and refresh the creation page to the Dashboard.
steps.success_signal: The Dashboard displays personal API keys and a cURL to try the sandbox.
steps.failure_or_wait: The guide does not publish a duration or sandbox-provisioning failure state.

steps.action: In the signed-in POST /users widget, paste the published `Marqeta User` JSON, send the request, and save the response `token`.
details: Interactive widgets appear only while signed in.
steps.success_signal: The Response body contains a user `token`.
steps.failure_or_wait: The guide does not enumerate widget request failures.

steps.action: In the signed-in GET /cardproducts widget, send the request and save the `token` for the card product named Reloadable Card.
steps.success_signal: The Response body contains the named card product and its token.
steps.failure_or_wait: The guide does not enumerate widget request failures.

steps.action: In the signed-in POST /cards widget, paste the published single-use-card JSON, replace the user and card-product token placeholders, send the request, and save the response card `token`.
details: The created single-use card becomes active immediately.
Creating another card terminates the previously active card.
steps.input: User token and Reloadable Card product token
steps.success_signal: The Response body contains the card `token`.
steps.failure_or_wait: The guide does not enumerate widget request failures.

steps.action: Add the published $10 authorization JSON to the simulate-a-transaction cURL and replace the card-token placeholder.
details: The request uses amount `10`, MID `123456890`, and network `VISA`.
No explicit card funding is required because the sandbox includes a Just-in-Time Funding source.
steps.input: Active card token and Dashboard-provided sample cURL
steps.success_signal: The cURL contains the real card token and the published authorization body.
steps.failure_or_wait: Using a terminated or incorrect card token prevents the intended simulation.

steps.action: Execute the simulate-a-transaction cURL against the public sandbox.
steps.success_signal: The transaction is created for the selected card and becomes available in Transaction Timeline.
steps.failure_or_wait: The Quick Start does not enumerate failure responses for this simulation.

steps.action: Open Transaction timeline in the Dashboard and select the active card token.
steps.input: Card with at least one simulated transaction
steps.success_signal: Transaction Timeline builds and displays the simulated transaction history for the card.
steps.failure_or_wait: The card list is available after at least one sandbox transaction; no additional refresh is required for new events.

branches.condition: Account creation has already created the public sandbox and user, as the account and quickstart text state
branches.path: Use the provisioned sandbox instead of selecting Create sandbox; reconcile any automatically created user with the Quick Start's separate create-user action.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Can remove the explicit sandbox-creation action and may change whether a user must be created, but the official sources do not resolve this against the next Create sandbox section.

branches.condition: Follow the Quick Start's separate Create sandbox section
branches.path: Select Create sandbox, wait for its readiness email and Dashboard refresh, then continue with the documented asset and transaction sequence.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an explicit provisioning action and wait; this is the route reconstructed in the candidate path.

branches.condition: Use sample cURLs instead of signed-in documentation widgets
branches.path: Copy the Dashboard application token, admin access token, and base URL, then use Basic authentication with the sample cURLs for user, card, and simulation requests.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses API credentials throughout instead of the signed-in widgets while preserving the simulated-transaction terminal.

friction_gates.description: Account creation requires API Terms of Service acceptance and displays a reCAPTCHA.
The password must have at least 16 characters with lowercase and uppercase letters, a number, and a symbol.
The developer must wait for and follow an account-verification email before signing in.
Official guidance conflicts on whether account creation already creates the sandbox and user or the developer must select Create sandbox afterward.
The explicit Create sandbox route requires waiting for a readiness email and Dashboard refresh.
Asset requests can use signed-in documentation widgets or Dashboard credentials with sample cURLs.

sources.title: Marqeta documentation
evidence_supported: Account, credentials, simulated transaction, and webhook sequence

sources.title: Core API Quick Start
evidence_supported: User, product, and card token sequence

sources.title: Marqeta.com Account Management
sections_used: Signing up for a Marqeta.com account

sources.title: Developer Tools
sections_used: Get API keys for your public sandbox

uncertainties.question: Does account creation itself provision the first sandbox and user, or must the developer select Create sandbox and create a user afterward?
uncertainties.impact: The number and order of required provisioning and asset-creation actions remain route-dependent.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Core API Quick Start and Account Management say account creation also creates a sandbox and user, but the next Quick Start sections separately instruct the developer to sign in, select Create sandbox, wait for readiness, and create a user.

excluded_after_success: Program funding source and GPA orders
Production card-program setup and Marqeta representative coordination
